name: TensorFlow
tagline: End-to-end platform for production ML at scale
description: Google's open-source ML framework supporting deep learning, neural networks, and production deployment across CPUs, GPUs, and TPUs with comprehensive tooling ecosystem.
use_cases_detailed: Train and deploy deep learning models at scale
Image classification and object detection pipelines
Edge deployment via TensorFlow Lite on mobile/IoT
anti_patterns: Not ideal for quick prototyping compared to PyTorch
Static graph mode can be confusing for beginners

name: PyTorch
tagline: Flexible deep learning framework for research and production
description: Meta's deep learning framework with dynamic computation graphs, seamless Python integration, and growing production deployment support via TorchScript and TorchServe.
use_cases_detailed: Rapid research prototyping with dynamic computation graphs
Training large language models and vision transformers
anti_patterns: TorchServe less mature than TF Serving for high-load production
Mobile deployment less streamlined than TF Lite
Larger community skew toward research vs production

name: scikit-learn
tagline: Simple and efficient tools for classical ML in Python
description: Python library for classical machine learning algorithms including classification, regression, clustering, dimensionality reduction with consistent API and excellent documentation.
use_cases_detailed: A/B test analysis and statistical modeling
anti_patterns: Not for deep learning or neural networks

name: JAX
tagline: Composable transformations of NumPy for high-performance ML research
description: Google's library for high-performance numerical computing with auto-differentiation, JIT compilation, and native GPU/TPU support built on XLA compiler.
use_cases_detailed: Cutting-edge ML research requiring custom gradient computation
Large-scale scientific simulation on TPU pods
anti_patterns: Steep learning curve for production engineers

name: Hugging Face Transformers
tagline: State-of-the-art NLP models, tokenizers, and pipelines
description: The largest ecosystem of pre-trained models for NLP, vision, audio, and multimodal tasks with easy fine-tuning, inference pipelines, and model sharing through the Hub.
use_cases_detailed: Fine-tune BERT for domain-specific text classification
Build RAG pipelines with sentence embeddings
anti_patterns: Model sizes can be very large - need GPU for decent speed
Not a full production serving solution by itself
Hub dependency for model downloads (needs internet first time)

name: spaCy
tagline: Industrial-strength NLP with pre-trained pipelines
description: Fast, production-ready NLP library for Python with pre-trained models for NER, POS tagging, dependency parsing, and text classification with excellent speed and accuracy.
use_cases_detailed: Extract medical entities from clinical notes
Build NER pipelines for legal document analysis
Fast text preprocessing for ML pipelines
anti_patterns: Less flexible than Transformers for custom architectures

name: OpenCV
tagline: Real-time computer vision library with 2500+ algorithms
description: The definitive open-source computer vision library supporting image processing, object detection, video analysis, camera calibration, and deep learning inference across platforms.
use_cases_detailed: Real-time video surveillance and object tracking
Industrial quality inspection on assembly lines
anti_patterns: Not a high-level ML framework - use with PyTorch/TF for DL
API can be inconsistent between versions

name: MLflow
tagline: Open-source AI platform for agents, LLMs & ML models
description: Comprehensive ML lifecycle platform for experiment tracking, model registry, deployment, LLM observability, prompt management, and AI Gateway with 100+ integrations.
use_cases_detailed: Track and compare ML experiments across teams
Version and deploy models to production
Manage AI Gateway for multi-provider LLM access
anti_patterns: Self-hosted tracking server needs PostgreSQL setup
UI can be slow with very large experiment counts

name: Gradio
tagline: Build ML web demos in minutes with Python
description: Python library for quickly creating customizable web interfaces for ML models, APIs, and data science workflows with shareable links and Hugging Face Spaces integration.
replaces: custom Flask apps for model demos
use_cases_detailed: Share ML model demos with stakeholders via link
Create interactive AI applications for non-technical users
Prototype and test models before production deployment
anti_patterns: Not suitable for complex multi-page apps

name: Streamlit
tagline: Build data apps in Python with minimal code
description: Python framework for creating interactive data applications and dashboards with simple script-based approach, reactive updates, and built-in widgets.
use_cases_detailed: Create ML model demos with file upload and predictions
Internal analytics tools for non-technical teams
LLM chatbot prototypes with conversation UI
anti_patterns: Not for complex multi-page production apps
Reruns entire script on interaction - slow for heavy compute
Limited layout control compared to proper frontend frameworks

name: Ollama
tagline: Run LLMs locally with one command
description: Local LLM inference server that downloads and runs open-weight models with a single command, exposing an OpenAI-compatible REST API for integration.
use_cases_detailed: Run LLMs locally for private/offline AI
Development environment with local AI models
anti_patterns: Needs decent GPU for large models

name: vLLM
tagline: High-throughput LLM serving engine
description: Production-grade LLM inference engine with PagedAttention for memory efficiency, continuous batching, speculative decoding, and OpenAI-compatible API.
use_cases_detailed: Serve LLMs in production with high throughput

name: LangChain
tagline: Framework for LLM-powered applications
description: The most popular framework for building LLM applications with chains, agents, tools, memory, retrievers, and extensive integrations for RAG and agentic workflows.
use_cases_detailed: Build RAG systems for document Q&A
Create AI agents with tool access
anti_patterns: Rapid API changes cause version friction
Can be overkill for simple LLM calls

name: CrewAI
tagline: Multi-agent AI orchestration framework
description: Framework for orchestrating role-playing AI agents that collaborate on complex tasks with defined roles, goals, and tools.
use_cases_detailed: Orchestrate research teams of AI agents
anti_patterns: High token consumption with verbose agent reasoning
Can get stuck in thinking loops

name: Milvus
tagline: Cloud-native vector DB for billion-scale AI search
description: Distributed vector database designed for billion-scale similarity search with GPU indexing, hybrid search, and multi-tenancy support.
use_cases_detailed: RAG retrieval backend for LLM applications
Semantic document search across knowledge base

name: Qdrant
tagline: High-performance vector similarity search engine
description: Vector database built in Rust for fast similarity search with filtering, payload storage, and distributed deployment.
use_cases_detailed: RAG retrieval backend for LLM applications
Semantic document search across knowledge base
anti_patterns: Needs separate storage for non-vector data

name: Chroma
tagline: AI-native open-source embedding database
description: Simple vector database designed for AI applications with easy Python/JS API, metadata filtering, and multi-modal support.
use_cases_detailed: RAG retrieval backend for LLM applications
Semantic document search across knowledge base

name: Weaviate
tagline: AI-native vector database with semantic modules
description: Vector database with built-in vectorization modules, hybrid search, GraphQL API, and multi-tenancy for production AI apps.
use_cases_detailed: RAG retrieval backend for LLM applications
Semantic document search across knowledge base
anti_patterns: Heavier than Chroma for simple use cases

name: Llama (Meta)
tagline: Meta's family of open-weight large language models
description: Open-weight LLM: Multiple sizes (7B-405B), multimodal, instruction-tuned, long context, fine-tunable, community ecosystem
use_cases_detailed: Self-hosted AI chatbot with full data privacy
anti_patterns: Smaller models trade quality for speed
Not as capable as frontier closed models for hardest tasks

name: DeepSeek V3
tagline: Frontier open-source reasoning LLM with MoE architecture
description: Open-weight LLM: Sparse attention, RL training, 85k+ agentic tasks, MoE, MIT license, GPT-5-level math benchmarks
use_cases_detailed: Self-hosted AI chatbot with full data privacy
anti_patterns: Smaller models trade quality for speed
Not as capable as frontier closed models for hardest tasks

name: Qwen3
tagline: Alibaba's open LLM family with hybrid thinking modes
description: Open-weight LLM: Hybrid thinking modes, 128K-262K context, MCP-optimized, multiple sizes, strong multilingual
use_cases_detailed: Self-hosted AI chatbot with full data privacy
anti_patterns: Smaller models trade quality for speed
Not as capable as frontier closed models for hardest tasks

name: Mistral
tagline: Efficient open-weight LLMs for edge and cloud
description: Open-weight LLM: MoE architecture, sliding window attention, function calling, multiple sizes, efficient inference
use_cases_detailed: Self-hosted AI chatbot with full data privacy
anti_patterns: Smaller models trade quality for speed
Not as capable as frontier closed models for hardest tasks

name: Phi-4 (Microsoft)
tagline: Compact but powerful reasoning model with MIT license
description: Open-weight LLM: MIT license (most permissive), strong reasoning, small footprint, Microsoft backing, commercially safe
use_cases_detailed: Self-hosted AI chatbot with full data privacy
anti_patterns: Smaller models trade quality for speed
Not as capable as frontier closed models for hardest tasks

name: Continue
tagline: Open-source AI code assistant for any IDE
description: AI coding assistant supporting any LLM and any IDE with code completion, chat, refactoring and custom context providers.
use_cases_detailed: Private AI coding assistant with local models
Custom context providers for company codebase
Multi-model code assistance in VS Code/JetBrains
anti_patterns: Less polished than GitHub Copilot UX

name: Tabby
tagline: Self-hosted AI coding assistant
description: Self-hosted AI code completion server built in Rust with support for multiple models, GPU acceleration, and IDE extensions.
use_cases_detailed: Private self-hosted code completion for enterprises
anti_patterns: Setup more complex than cloud alternatives

name: PostgreSQL
tagline: The world's most advanced open-source relational database
description: Enterprise-grade relational database with JSONB, extensions ecosystem (PostGIS, TimescaleDB, pgvector), MVCC, full ACID compliance, and rich SQL feature set.
use_cases_detailed: Primary transactional database for web applications
Vector similarity search with pgvector for RAG
JSONB document storage as MongoDB alternative
anti_patterns: Not ideal for pure key-value workloads at massive scale
Write-heavy append-only workloads better served by Cassandra

name: MySQL
tagline: World's most popular open-source relational database
description: Widely-used relational database with InnoDB storage engine, replication, partitioning, and JSON support. The backbone of LAMP stack web development.
anti_patterns: Less feature-rich than PostgreSQL for advanced SQL
JSON support weaker than PostgreSQL JSONB

name: Redis
tagline: In-memory data structure store for sub-millisecond latency
description: In-memory key-value store supporting strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, streams, and modules for JSON, search, time-series, and graph data structures.
use_cases_detailed: Application cache layer for database query results
anti_patterns: Data must fit in RAM - expensive at scale
Not for primary data storage (persistence is secondary)
License changed from BSD - check RSALv2 terms

tagline: MySQL fork with enhanced features and community governance
description: MySQL fork with enhanced features and community governance

description: Embedded serverless database in a single file
